title: Data Injection via Unvalidated Input
description: Without details on the system’s data handling processes, it’s highly likely that user-supplied input is not adequately validated or sanitized before being used in database queries or other operations. This creates an opportunity for attackers to inject malicious code (e.g., SQL injection) or manipulate data, potentially leading to data corruption, unauthorized access, or system compromise.
affected_component: All Data Processing Components (Web Application, API, Database)
attack_vector: Injection of malicious SQL queries, JavaScript, or other code through vulnerable input fields.
mitigations: Implement robust input validation and sanitization techniques for all user-supplied data.
Use parameterized queries or prepared statements to prevent SQL injection.
Employ a Web Application Firewall (WAF) to filter malicious requests.
